user_query:
Please set up a local registry of PostgreSQL tools and resources that stores its data under output/registry, then populate and report on it as follows:

1) Initialize the registry and confirm it uses output/registry for its data.
2) Read input/resources.csv (columns: name, category, url, description). Add each row as a single entry to the registry with the exact text payload formatted as: "<name> | <category> | <url> | <description>".
3) After all entries are added, list the registry and save the full listing to output/list.txt.
4) Read input/queries.txt (one search term per line). For each term (trimmed, case-insensitive search):
   - Run a search against the registry.
   - Save the exact search output to a separate file at output/search/<term_normalized>.txt where <term_normalized> is the term lowercased with spaces replaced by underscores.
5) Export the full registry to output/export/all.txt.
6) Build a JSON report at output/search_summary.json with this structure:
   {
     "total_entries": <number>,
     "queries": [
       {"term": "<original term>", "matches": <count of matching lines>}, ...
     ]
   }
   Notes:
   - total_entries must equal the number of rows in input/resources.csv that you added.
   - matches for each term must equal the number of lines in the corresponding output/search/<term_normalized>.txt that are real hits (do not count any line that says "Not found").
7) Write a status file to output/status.json with:
   {
     "data_dir": "output/registry",
     "ready": true
   }
   Use the tool's status to determine readiness.

Make sure all files are written exactly to the specified output/ paths and that each non-"Not found" search result line corresponds to an entry present in the exported registry.
